input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].type}' 2>/dev/null)
  ACTIVE=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.active}' 2>/dev/null)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) status=$STATUS active=$ACTIVE failed=$FAILED"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -qE "Complete|Failed"; then break; fi
  sleep 5
done

file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        # Broken baseline (Law 5): the job is configured with only 256m of
        # driver/executor memory. In local[2] mode the executor runs inside
        # the driver JVM, and a 256m heap is below Spark's own minimum
        # (~471MB reserved system memory), so the submit fails
        # deterministically with a "must be at least ... increase heap size"
        # memory error — the fault the prompt describes. The agent must raise
        # the memory (e.g. 1g for both --driver-memory and
        # spark.executor.memory) and re-run. Do NOT ship the remediated
        # values (1g / AQE flags) here: they are exactly the fix_applied()
        # markers the oracle greps for, and a pre-fixed baseline lets an idle
        # agent pass.
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 256m \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=256m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 2Gi

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue clearly. Lines 40-41 have `--driver-memory 256m` and `--conf spark.executor.memory=256m`. I need to raise both to `1g`.

Let me also optionally add AQE skew handling as mentioned in the task.

content.text: Found it. Lines 40–41 have `256m` for both driver and executor memory. I'll raise both to `1g` and add AQE skew handling.

content.content: The file /mydata/karma/cases/spark/spark_etl_skew_oom/resource/etl-job.yaml has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_use_result.originalFile:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        # Broken baseline (Law 5): the job is configured with only 256m of
        # driver/executor memory. In local[2] mode the executor runs inside
        # the driver JVM, and a 256m heap is below Spark's own minimum
        # (~471MB reserved system memory), so the submit fails
        # deterministically with a "must be at least ... increase heap size"
        # memory error — the fault the prompt describes. The agent must raise
        # the memory (e.g. 1g for both --driver-memory and
        # spark.executor.memory) and re-run. Do NOT ship the remediated
        # values (1g / AQE flags) here: they are exactly the fix_applied()
        # markers the oracle greps for, and a pre-fixed baseline lets an idle
        # agent pass.
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 256m \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=256m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 2Gi

lines: echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"

content.text: Now delete the failed job, apply the fix, and optionally scale workers.

input.command:
kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-etl --replicas=4 2>&1
kubectl set env deployment/spark-worker -n spark-etl SPARK_WORKER_MEMORY=2G 2>&1
input.description: Scale workers and increase worker memory

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].type}' 2>/dev/null)
  ACTIVE=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.active}' 2>/dev/null)
  SUCCEEDED=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>/dev/null)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job etl-job -n spark-etl -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) status=$STATUS active=$ACTIVE succeeded=$SUCCEEDED failed=$FAILED"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -qE "Complete|Failed"; then break; fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Watch job until completion or failure

description: Watch job until completion or failure
